data.title: AMZY: Good Yield, But No Return Policy On Losses
data.description:
YieldMax AMZN Option Income Strategy ETF (AMZY) is a self-described income ETF that often boasts a yield around 50%. I might not call that "too good to be true," but I believe this fund is better for those who want to bet on Amazon (AMZN) and have an easier time monetizing capital gains. Since the stock is fairly valued, so too is this ETF.

Concept of the Fund

Traditional income investments, at their most basic, take an investor's capital and invest it to produce yield without compromising the principal. BDCs typically do this with secured loans to smaller companies, which allow them greater covenants and higher coupons. When REITs do this, the real estate is the principal, and the rent is the yield. There are many ways to produce yield with relatively stable principal.

AMZY instead uses a covered call strategy on shares of AMZN. Under a traditional covered call strategy, the underlying stock is owned, and calls are written to produce yield. As stocks are volatile and aren't secured like debt, such a strategy would need to be employed with a sound company for the principal to be considered secure.

YieldMax, however, adds another layer to this strategy to produce synthetic covered calls. As the prospectus explains:

To achieve a synthetic long exposure to the Underlying Security, the Fund will buy the Underlying Security’s call options and, simultaneously, sell the Underlying Security’s put options to try to replicate the price movements of the Underlying Security.

Instead of owning AMZN outright, they use those derivative positions to provide the same coverage. In the image above, I've indicated how most of the capital is in Treasuries or a cash-equivalent position. This covers the puts they sell, and the proceeds of those puts are used to purchase calls, which can then be used to write calls at higher strike prices, creating the income.

There are some subtle, important differences to this approach, compared to owning AMZN. Before I address those, the key takeaway is that AMZY is an income fund that depends on a successful options writing strategy.

Dividends and Price Returns

When assessing an income fund, the first measure of success is their ability to provide said income. Shortly after it launched in 2023, AMZY has done just that since September of that year.

The chart above shows monthly dividends being paid, ranging between $0.50 and $1.00 per share in most cases. These payments have provided the large yield that I mentioned before.

One will note that the dividend seems to rise and fall in cycles, and this depends heavily on the short-term performance of AMZN, as that affects the premiums earned from writing options. This would be true for traditional and synthetic covered calls.

The price chart above shows that as AMZN gained in certain periods, so too did AMZY's dividends. A rising stock price naturally increases the value of call premiums sold. Yet, the price return of AMZY has also declined over time. When AMZN experiences a drawdown, AMZY not only declines, but NAV never seems to return to that point.

This has more to do with the puts AMZY writes than the calls. As puts obligate AMZY to purchase shares at a strike price, this means they can be forced into a loss if the stock declines. While traditional covered calls would not require this and give them more room to bounce back, this often forces such funds to close out losing positions. Without a gain on the trade, any dividends not covered by the Treasury yields must be paid out of capital for the time.

As covered calls limit upside, this also means recoveries to NAV do not occur easily.

When dividends are reinvested, total returns for AMZY approximate those of AMZN, but there is a slight underperformance driven by the friction on this fund from these eventual losses, plus the 1.17% expense ratio.

Outlook and Valuation

Looking at historical results, it's hard for AMZY shareholders to hate what they got. Whether it's the income or the total return, it's above-average results. We still ought to consider the context and how that affects the risk profile.

Above is AMZN's price history from the last five years. Notice the prolonged dive it took after Federal Reserve began hiking interest rates in 2022. AMZY was launched as these hikes came to a halt. This not only reduced the near-term chances of NAV loss, it ensured the Treasury positions that cover their puts would have more yield to support their income. As even a few rate cuts have occurred, AMZY has managed to live in an easier monetary environment for its type of strategy than if it had launched in 2021.

Independent of what the Fed does and other macro forces, they do benefit from the fact that they are tied to the upside of Amazon.

The company enjoys a strong balance sheet, with over $90B in liquidity to cover $50B in debt.

It also tends to produce strong free cash flow, further strengthening its liquidity over time. These things are to say that major or sustained NAV losses for AMZY are not likely because AMZN's fundamentals are solid. As it's primarily a consumer retail business, I also think these fundamentals are much more tied to predictable habits, not based on far-flung visions of the future that you see with many tech stocks and the volatility that comes with that.

Where we might worry is on valuation. With TTM earnings of $70.6B, the stock trades for a multiple of 34. It's not as large as many of the Mag 7 stocks, but AMZN isn't priced very cheaply either. It may not be overvalued, but it doesn't seem undervalued either. Multiple contractions, even to 25 (still not small), are plausible in the life of AMZY and something that would affect NAV per share. The higher the multiple, the more sensitive the stock is to changes in growth rates, real or perceived.

While the downside doesn't strike me as huge, I don't think there's a compelling risk-reward asymmetry. As AMZN seems fairly valued at its multiple, AMZY necessarily seems fairly valued as well.

Conclusion

This fund has room to provide good returns, but I think there are scenarios in which income investors could see their NAV and principal cut without room to recover. AMZY seems better for investors who want to bet on a period of upside for AMZN and have those gains automated into distributions that require less individual trades.

When AMZN is undervalued, AMZY is a buy. Because a P/E of 34 is fair even for strong growth, I think the same must follow for AMZY, and I give it a neutral Hold rating.

data.title: REV Group: Its Robust Growth, Liquidity, And Market Drivers Justify Upside Potential
data.description:
Just three months after my last coverage of REV Group, Inc. (NYSE:REVG), we saw a lot of interesting changes in this stock. After surging by over 20% in a short period, a revisit is necessary to reevaluate the stock at this level. Yet, I believe the stock is still trading at a reasonable level, justified by its robust fundamentals and growth prospects. This is supported by price ratios updated based on its most recent performance. Technicals adhere to it as momentum remains strong despite some early divergence tendencies.

REVG Q3 2025: Sustained Strength And Recovery

REV Group, Inc. closed the third quarter with a much stronger performance than in my previous articles. During Q3 2025, we saw more improvements in both business segments. The operating revenue amounted to $644.9M, up by 11.3% YoY from $579.4M. It also increased by 2.5% from Q2 2025, which shows sustained growth amid market volatility.

The Specialty Vehicles segment remained the jewel of the crown as segment revenues rose by 11.8%. This also comprised 74% of the total amount. If we exclude the impact of Bus Manufacturing Businesses, the YoY increase will be much higher at 24.6%. This shows a robust performance in this segment, driven both by increased shipments and pricing. At the end of the quarter, backlog reached $4.3B versus $4.1B YoY.

Meanwhile, its RV segment enjoyed revenue growth for the first time after its softer demand in many quarters. This was mainly due to the motorhome market recovery as shipments rose substantially. I will discuss this market view in the next section. Another factor that drove its surprising recovery was its strategic pricing, allowing it to offset the impact of fewer backlogs and new order intakes.

What’s more interesting is that REVG also enhanced its efficiency amid its increased operating capacity and production volume. Although inflation and tariffs increased cost pressures, revenue growth completely offset them. As a result, the operating income doubled. The operating margin increased a lot from 4.9% to 8.8%. This means that for every $1 revenue, REVG generated $0.09 compared to $0.05 in the comparative quarter.

Drivers

In recent months, we have seen the advent of motorhome recovery as shipments improved on a YoY basis. As an RV company with a high concentration on motorhomes rather than towables, REVG may benefit from these trends. I also think this is the reason for the improved YoY change in its RV sales. Since my last coverage, motorhome shipments have increased substantially. If we can remember, my last article covered April 2025 motorhome shipments. After its slight YoY weakness, motorhomes bounced back in May as shipments reached 3,153 units, up by 6.1% YoY from 2,973. This continued in June as shipment growth accelerated to 22.4% YoY to 2,800 units. In July, motorhome shipments still enjoyed double-digit growth at 15.3% YoY to 2,947 units. Considering the trajectory, motorhomes may regain their footing this second half due to several reasons.

One is that seasonal demand spikes in summer and autumn may boost more shipments today. Second, inventory restocking before the actual materialization of new tariffs may be expedited to manage costs and expenses. Third, inflation is still stubborn, which may affect travel spending this summer and autumn. This may be supported by the lower oil prices, which means lower fuel expenses. With that in mind, RVs may become a good alternative to hotels and international flights. I believe it’s possible that people will shift to domestic road travel to save costs. In a study, it was found that many Americans downsized their summer spending by about 25% YoY. In connection with this, RVing is becoming more popular again as many Americans try to fit travel into their budget. In a recent survey, over 50% of Americans shifted to road trips via personal or rental vehicles. More interestingly, Autumn kicked off with 7.4M Americans spending Labor Day on their RVs, a slight increase from the previous year.

Another driver and risk mitigant of REVG is its robust liquidity position. With its solid growth and margins, REVG enjoys high operating cash inflows. This is more than enough to cover its capex. In fact, it generated an FCF of $136M, which means that it converted 8% of its sales into free cash using the FCF/Sales Ratio. With that, it’s not surprising that it does not have to issue more debt to cover its dividends and share repurchases for the period.

Meanwhile, REVG maintains a low debt level, which means that it generates enough cash to cover its operations. In addition, its debt maturity profile remains long since the remaining amount will mature in 2030. More interestingly, its Net Debt/EBITDA is only 0.3x. This means that REVG only needs a few months to repay all borrowings at once even if it doesn’t have to. Hence, it has enough capital buffer and earnings to sustain its capacity while covering borrowings and dividends.

Valuation

After the price surged by over 20%, I updated REVG’s valuation to determine if buying at this level is still a good idea. I still used P/E and P/FCF to assess the stock price. Again, the stock appears cheap relative to previous years. Using P/E, the stock trades at 24.55x its EPS versus the five-year average of 27.75x. If we use this to derive the fair value, the TP will be $58.54. The same goes for P/FCF as the stock now only trades at 11.54x versus the average of 15.13x. Using this, the TP is $67.95. Either way, there is still upside potential in this stock.

P/E Ratio P/FCF Ratio
2020 51.11 19.61
2021 20.26 7.24
2022 51.76 10.96
2023 16.74 5.07
2024 4.78 32.78
Average 28.93 15.13
2025 TTM 24.55 11.54
TP $61.04 $67.95

Technicals

REVG’s momentum is still bullish, and that’s valid in my view. After all, its robust fundamentals and valuation can justify it. The price is still below the 50-day and 200-day SMA, which shows that recent buying volumes still support the uptrend. In addition, the 50-day SMA is still increasing, which means that market sentiments are still generally optimistic.

More interestingly, MACD and RSI are still consistent with the price trend, so we cannot detect any bearish divergence yet. The MACD line and histogram are still increasing and moving within positive territory. This means that buying volume is still increasing and higher than selling volume. My only concern right now is that the stock is already overbought. I understand this reaction following the release of its Q3 performance. But before immediately buying, know that profit-takers may act and lead to some downward force on the price. A dip is possible, which will open new entry opportunities. But as investors of REVG, it’s still a good stock to keep in our portfolio.

Conclusion

REV Group, Inc. maintains robust growth and margins, supported by its recovery in the RV segment. Its liquidity is also high, positioning it well towards growth opportunities in the RV market. In addition, the stock remains reasonably priced with upside potential. Technicals are still bullish, but newcomers may wish to wait for a dip as the stock is now overbought. I’m reiterating my buy rating on this stock.

data.title: MARA Holdings: Leveraging Bitcoin's Potential Surge
data.description:
Introduction & Investment Thesis

I am bullish on MARA Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: NASDAQ:MARA), driven by Bitcoin’s price estimate of $200,000 by 2030. Before I arrived at this angle, I first went to look at the company’s standing in terms of bitcoin mining. What I am finding is that in the latest quarter, MARA’s Q2’2025 bitcoin mining increased by 3% YoY to 2,358 BTC. This increase was seen after an increased hash rate by 6% from 54.3 EH/s in Q1’2025 to 57.4 EH/s. This indicates that the company has high computational power dedicated to mining and securing more blockchain in Bitcoin. After examining MARA’s edge in Bitcoin mining, I also notice that its revenues in the Q2’2025 increased by 64% YoY to $238.5 million. Moreover, the company’s net income surged by a whopping 505% YoY to $808 million, driven by gains in Bitcoin appreciation. Between April 2025 to June 2025, Bitcoin appreciated from $76,000 to $110,000, representing a 44.74% gain, which contributed to MARA’s gain. Therefore, looking at the historical price returns of BTC and MARA, I find that as Bitcoin gains, MARA revenues soar even after recording a minimal revenue increase YoY, as seen in Q2’2025.

This prompts me to find out what is there for MARA in BTC price projection reaching $200,000 by 2030. Given my thesis above, I will now delve into why I believe BTC performance will influence MARA profitability in the future, which is why I am rating this ticker as a buy.

Company Brief

MARA Holdings provides technology solutions that optimize data center operations, such as firmware for Bitcoin miners and liquid immersion cooling. The company was incorporated in 2010 and is headquartered in Hallandale Beach in Florida.

The Market Outlook

Before I explain how MARA is positioning itself to maintain a consistent mining capability to make the most from the Bitcoin gain, let me get to what makes this $200,000 price seem close by 2030.

Firstly, one of the drivers is the creation of value from the institutional adoption. A good example is Trump’s administration executive order creating a leeway for American citizens to invest in Bitcoin as their retirement plans, since it’s already classified among the US store of value. Over 90 million Americans who did not have an opportunity to participate in digital assets directly through their retirement plans can now access this alternative investment programme. Government involvement in Bitcoin invokes confidence in Bitcoin as a digital asset, which could suggest that there is potential of upside potential from this institutional optimism. Also, when I look at the US Spot Bitcoin ETF that was launched in 2024, which opened access to institutional Bitcoin capital, it has so far generated around $5 billion to $10 billion daily on active days by Q3’2025.

Secondly, regulatory clarity around US crypto is undergoing a significant shift. For instance, the GENIUS Act, which was signed into law on July 18, 2025 is regulates stable coin payments. After the GENIUS Act (Guiding and Establishing National Innovation for the US), jurisdictional ambiguities that limited access to Bitcoin have been resolved. Following the Act, the federal regime payment of stablecoin requires reporting 100% of their reserve banking and monthly disclosures. This regulatory initiative aims to improve trust in digital coins such as Bitcoin.

Still on Bitcoin gaining leverage and a store of value, I am also looking at it from an angle of possible crypto credit cards driven by halving, which is managing its inflation and scarcity to shape its long-term value. I previously introduced the executive order requiring organizations to start considering alternative stores of value, a trend that is evident in 2025 Bitcoin ownership, where businesses continue owning most Bitcoins.

MARA’s Position In Bitcoin Optimism

Now, with this picture in mind, I will explain to you how I see MARA positioning itself for this gain in Bitcoin value. I am looking at this from two angles, including its edge in bitcoin mining capability and how it is gaining as Bitcoin gains in value.

Let’s begin with its edge in Bitcoin mining. MARA has an edge at its current 54.3 EH/s to 57.4 EH/s. This makes MARA a leading company in terms of hash rate, to means that it has a high chance of being the first company to solve new blocks. Having a high hash rate shows that MARA’s computational power is higher than its competitors, and this makes it capable of solving complex mathematical problems much faster.

Energy consumption is a major concern affecting earnings rewards in Bitcoin mining, but MARA has insulated itself from this cost by transitioning power reliance to its own power production. The company now owns 114W wind power in Texas, which adds up to 70% of its total fleet it owns and operates. This energy independence is a major driver of more profit margins that would be consumed by power volatility. As of Q2’2025, the company increased its mining fleet by 26% YoY, which consumes around 18.3 J/TH, which is among the industry’s lowest. MARA remains a leading Bitcoin miner compared to its competitors, on a month-by-month basis, which is a good leverage point as the Bitcoin price shows a high chance of an upside.

Now, this brings me to my second view on how MARA will benefit from Bitcoin gain trading, aiming to reach $200,000 by 2030. Recall that in my investment thesis, I demonstrated how the price return trajectory of BTC causes a similar trajectory in MARA Holdings’ price return. For example, from June 30, 2024, BTC was trading at $62,668, and the value has increased to $111,358 currently. This continuous increase in Bitcoin has a significant impact on MARA as it continues to mine more Bitcoins and hold them compared to competitors’ selling rate.

I believe MARA is maintaining this holding strategy, looking up to the projected Bitcoin gains demonstrated above. The hint that directs my thought process along this perspective is the recent Q2’2025, which reported a substantial net income of $808.2 million from a loss of $199.7 million in Q2’2024. This gain was driven by the increase in Bitcoin’s fair value of $1.2 billion from its Bitcoin holding as the price of Bitcoin gained during the quarter.

Financials And Valuation

In the last 5 years, MARA revenue growth YoY has been averaging above 100% but 2022 was an exception when the revenue growth was -26.02% due to a sharp decline in Bitcoin, which was selling at $16,400 at the time from $64,800. Since then, Bitcoin’s price has been increasing, and this is equally portrayed in MARA’s revenues, which have been beyond double digits.

When you look at the revenue growth YoY, FY2023 saw an increase by 229.08% but has been going down to the current TTM of 41.32% and I am equating this to increasing Bitcoin network difficulty, value and hash price. The company is addressing this through increasing its hash rate, which currently stands at 57.4 EH/s, and moving away from the national grid to its own wind power generation. As a result, the company has become the leading Bitcoin miner and holds the most Bitcoins. This is an edge that MARA has to leverage to capitalize on Bitcoin increase in Bitcoin price.

Let’s move to profitability. In the same timeframe, the company’s profitability has regained significantly from a loss of $694.022 million in FY2022 due to Bitcoin’s loss in value. Since then, as Bitcoin regained its value, the company’s profitability has been on an uptrend, with the current TTM net income at $678.78 million. The company

Moving on to valuation, I will use the P/S ratio metric to compare that of its competitors, Riot Platforms, Inc. (NASDAQ: RIOT) and Hut 8 Corp. (NASDAQ: HUT). MARA, with a P/S ratio of 6.63x, is undervalued, given its high hash rate of 57.4 EH/s. Compared to MARA hash rate, which is a major driver in accessing more Bitcoin blockchains, the latest hash rate for RIOT is 35.4 EH/s with a P/S ratio of 8.26x and HUT’s hash rate is 9.3 EH/s at a P/S ratio of 19.43x. This shows how much the market has undervalued MARA.

I am also learning that even without selling its Bitcoins, MARA maintains a positive revenue growth YoY of 41.32% compared to RIOT, which is selling its Bitcoins and currently remaining with 19,287 BTC at revenue YoY growth of 93.46%. On the other side, HUT, which has held its Bitcoins, its revenue YoY is at -5.64%. This means that if MARA were to sell its Bitcoins now, it would probably record the highest revenue growth YoY, but instead holds them for future value discussed above, and this is why I reiterate my bullish rating.

Even with the company’s holding strategy to potentially benefit from high prices, I see a significant upside potential here. To estimate my price target by 2026, I will start by projecting the company’s financial performance by the said year. In my estimations, I will assume a revenue growth rate of 55% for 2025 and 35% for 2026. This reflects its holding initiative while maintaining strong growth. For profitability, I expect its net income to expand slightly from the 2024 82.46% to about 86% in 2026. With these assumptions, I am arriving at an estimated revenue of $1.37 billion by 2026 which is very close to the consensus estimates of $1.33 billion.

Considering the company’s undervaluation, I believe the company will reward this company with a better P/S as the Bitcoin value keeps growing which as demonstrated earlier leads to a higher price for this stock. For this reason, I will assume a forward P/S of 10x. This is between its peers which represents my optimism and the fact that MARA may not fetch a higher PS as HUT who has a stronger sales growth because it’s liquidating most of its Bitcoins. With these assumptions, I am projecting a price of about $37.07 per share by 2026 marking an upside potential of more than 133%. Given this potential alpha, I think my buy recommendation is justified.

Investment risks

Volatility of Bitcoin – Bitcoin’s volatility means that in a massive bearish market, it would significantly downgrade its held Bitcoins, which would minimize its revenues.

Increasing hash rate – Maintaining a hash rate above 50 EH/s will require MARA to continually upgrade its infrastructure and energy sourcing, which would continue to eat up its mining rewards, thus reducing overall revenues.

Conclusion

I am reiterating my buy rating on MARA, driven Buy its high Bitcoin holding and hash rate of 57.4EH/s. The Bitcoin mining industry is still not yet exhausted, and given its edge in mining and the projected increase in Bitcoin value, the company stands a better position to gain.
